summary.what: Added --var key=value flag to draft command for caller-supplied template variables
summary.why: Namespacing under {{vars.key}} unlocks durable ADR numbering for decision-log without timbers owning a counter — keeps draft templates as pure renderers
summary.how: New repeatable --var flag on cobra command parses into map[string]string, threaded through RenderContext.Vars, substituted after built-ins in internal/draft/render.go. Unknown keys remain literal, matching existing token behavior. Extracted prepareRender helper to keep runDraftRender under cyclop limit.

notes: Considered putting the counter in .timbers/state.json but rejected — two sources of truth means they can drift, and regenerating an ADR file would orphan the counter. The output file is inherently durable (version-controlled markdown); making the caller extract the offset from it means one source of truth. Also considered a generic --var without namespacing; namespacing keeps built-in vars like {{entry_count}} sacred so callers can't accidentally shadow them.
